D4 Colorimetry L4 Analyze Reference

2° vs 10° Standard Observer

slide the wavelength and compare the 1931 2° and 1964 10° colour-matching functions side by side

A 2° field falls on the fovea; a 10° field spreads onto rod-rich, macular-pigment-free retina. The CIE tabulated a colour-matching function set for each.

Solid curves are the 1931 2° observer, dashed the 1964 10°. Slide the wavelength marker and read all six values — the largest divergence sits in the blue, where macular pigment screens the foveal 2° field.
